archetype: Real-Time Electromagnetic Interference (EMI) Spectral Fingerprinting & Susceptor Localization Engine for Shielded Medical Device Maintenance
domain: Clinical Biomedical Engineering & Electromagnetic Compatibility (EMC) Compliance
description: A specialized system that ingests high-fidelity spectrum analyzer data from shielded rooms and MRI suites to automatically identify, classify, and geolocate microscopic electromagnetic interference sources that compromise sensitive life-support equipment. It is used by biomedical engineers and RF safety officers to diagnose intermittent device failures that standard multimeters miss, requiring real-time FFT processing of GHz-range signals against a database of known medical device emission signatures.
rules: MUST process raw IQ (In-phase/Quadrature) data streams from Vector Signal Analyzers via SCPI or TCP/IP interfaces with sub-millisecond latency to capture transient RF bursts.
MUST strictly adhere to IEC 60601-1-2 (Medical electrical equipment - Electromagnetic compatibility) and FCC Part 15 Class A/B limits for emission thresholds.
MUST correlate spectral anomalies with facility power grid harmonics and HVAC vibration data to distinguish between external RF intrusion and internal ground-loop noise.
MUST implement cryptographic signing of all diagnostic logs to maintain chain-of-custody for regulatory audits and liability investigations.
MUST support dynamic frequency hopping algorithms to avoid false positives from legitimate diagnostic equipment (e.g., pulse oximeters) operating within the same frequency band.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use simple threshold-based alerting; EMI is non-linear and requires probabilistic spectral pattern recognition to distinguish noise from critical interference.
DO NOT rely on asynchronous batch processing; transient RF events causing device malfunction last microseconds and require real-time stream processing.
DO NOT ignore the physical layer context; analyzing spectral data without mapping it to the specific room topology and shielding integrity leads to false localization.
key_technical_challenges: Executing real-time Fast Fourier Transforms (FFT) on 100+ MHz bandwidths with <10ms latency on edge hardware to catch intermittent bursts.
Developing a machine learning model capable of distinguishing between benign environmental RF noise and specific interference signatures that cause pacemaker or ventilator failure.
Handling massive time-series datasets from multiple simultaneous sensor nodes across large hospital campuses while maintaining strict temporal synchronization.

recommended_stack.language: Rust — Essential for memory safety and deterministic real-time performance when handling high-speed DSP streams without garbage collection pauses.
recommended_stack.framework: Axum + Tonic — Provides high-performance async HTTP/gRPC services for streaming IQ data from hardware instruments to the analysis engine.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB — Optimized for high-write ingestion of time-series spectral data with built-in hypertable partitioning for long-term compliance archiving.
recommended_stack.cache: null — Pure stream processing is required; caching introduces unacceptable latency for transient burst detection.
recommended_stack.message_broker: NATS JetStream — Offers wire-level performance and at-least-once delivery for decoupling sensor ingestion from heavy spectral analysis workloads.
special_services: Regulatory Compliance Report Generator (PDF/A compliant with digital signatures)
